(3) It shall come into force on the first day of July, 1887.

(3) Any enactment or document referring to the Bengal Civil Courts Act,

shall be construed to refer to this Act or to the corresponding portion thereof.

Repealed_Clauses: 5. [Repealed by the Decentralization Act, 1914 (Act No. IV of 1914).]

Content:
6. (1) Whenever the office of District Judge or District] Judge is
vacant by reason of the death, resignation or removal of the Judge or other
cause, or whenever an increase in the number of District or District
Judges has been made under the provisions of section 4, the Government
or, as the case
may be, the High Court Division may fill up the vacancy or appoint the
Additional District Judges or District Judges.
(2) Nothing in this section shall be construed to prevent a Government from
appointing a District Judge or District Judge to discharge, for such
period as it thinks fit, in addition to the functions devolving on him as such
District Judge or District Judge, all or any of the functions of another
District Judge or District Judge, as the case may be.

Repealed_Clauses: 7. [Omitted by the Government of India (Adaptation of Indian Laws) Order, 1937.]

Content:
8.(1) When the business pending before any District Judge requires the aid
of District Judges for its speedy disposal, the Government may,
(2) District] Judges so appointed shall discharge any of the
functions of a District Judge which the District Judge may assign to them,
and, in the discharge of those functions, they shall exercise the same powers
as the District Judge.

9. Subject to the superintendence of the High Court Division, the District
Judge shall have administrative control over all the Civil Courts under this Act
within the local limits of his jurisdiction.

Section_Title: Courts Temporary charge of District Court
Content:
10. (1) In the event of the death, resignation or removal of the District Judge,
or of his being incapacitated by illness or otherwise for the performance of
his duties, or of his absence from the place at which his Court is held, the
District Judges, or, if an District] Judge is not
present at that place, the senior District Judge present thereat, shall,
without relinquishing his ordinary duties, assume charge of the office of the
District Judge, and shall continue in charge thereof until the office is resumed
by the District Judge or assumed by an officer appointed thereto.
(2) While in charge of the office of the District Judge, the District]
Judge or District Judge, as the case may be, may, subject to any
rules which the High Court Division may make in this behalf, exercise any of
the powers of the District Judge.

Section_Title: Transfer of proceedings on vacation of office of
Content:
11. (1) In the event of the death, resignation or removal of a District]
Judge, or of his being incapacitated by illness or otherwise for the
performance of his duties, or of his absence from the place at which his
Court is held, the District Judge may transfer all or any of the proceedings
pending in the Court of the District Judge either to his own Court or
to any Court under his administrative control competent to dispose of them.
(3) Provided that the District Judge may re-transfer to the Court of the
District] Judge or his successor any proceedings transferred under sub-
section (1) to his own or any other Court.
(4) For the purposes of proceedings which are not pending in the Court of
the District Judge on the occurrence of an event referred to in sub-
section (1), and with respect to which that Court has exclusive jurisdiction,
the District Judge may exercise all or any of the jurisdiction of that Court.

Repealed_Clauses: 12. [Omitted by the Government of India (Adaptation of Indian Laws) Order, 1937.]

Section_Title: Power to fix local limits of jurisdiction
Content:
13. (1) The Government may, by notification in the official Gazette, fix
and alter the local limits of the jurisdiction of any Civil Court under this
(2) If the same local jurisdiction is assigned to two or more
District] Judges or to two or more Judges or
Judges], the District Judge may assign to each of them such civil
business cognizable by the District] Judge or
Judge or Judge], as the case may be, as, subject to any general
or special orders of the High Court Division, he thinks fit.
(3) When civil business arising in any local area is assigned by the
District Judge under sub-section (2) to one of two or more
District] Judges or to one of two or more Judges or
Judges, a decree or order passed by the District] Judge
or Judge or Judge] shall not be invalid by
reason only of the case in which it was made having arisen wholly or in
part in a place beyond the local area if that place is within the local limits
fixed by the Government under sub-section (I).
Judge or Senior Judge or Judge], as the case may be,
within the meaning of this section.
(5) The present local limits of the jurisdiction of every Civil Court under
this Act shall be deemed to have been fixed under this section.

14. (1) The Government may, by notification in the official Gazette, fix and
alter the place or places at which any Civil Court under this Act is to be held.
(2) All places at which any such Courts are now held shall be deemed to
have been fixed under this section.

15. (1) Subject to such orders as may be made by the Government the High
Court Division shall prepare a list of days to be observed in each year as
closed holidays in the Civil Courts.
(2) The list shall be published in the official Gazette.
(3) A judicial act done by a Civil Court on a day specified in the list shall not
be invalid by reason only of its having been done on that day.

16. Every Civil Court under this Act shall use a seal of such form and
dimensions as are prescribed by the Government.

Section_Title: Courts Continuance of proceedings of Courts
Content:
17. (1) Where any Civil Court under this Act has from any cause ceased to
have jurisdiction with respect to any case, any proceedings in relation to that
case which, if that Court had not ceased to have jurisdiction, might have
been had therein may be had in the Court to which the business of the
former Court has been transferred.
(2) Nothing in this section applies to cases for which provision is made in
sections 36, 37 and 114 of, and rule 1 of Order XLVII in Schedule I to the
Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, or in any other enactment for the time being
in force.

18. Save as otherwise provided by any enactment for the time being in force,
the jurisdiction of a District Judge or District Judge extends, subject
to the provisions of section 15 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 to all
original suits for the time being cognizable by Civil Courts.

19. (1) Save as otherwise provided by any enactment for the time
Civil 5a
being in force, the jurisdiction of a Senior Judge and Civil
Judge shall extend to all suits of which the value does not exceed 25
(twenty five) lac Taka and 15 (fifteen) lac Taka respectively.
(2) The District Judge shall transfer the suit or proceeding pending
before the Joint District Judge Court or the Senior Judge Court to
the competent court within 90 (ninety) days from the date of enforcement
of the Civil Courts (Amendment) Act, 2021.
(3) If any suit or proceeding is transferred under sub-section (2), the suit
or proceeding shall start from the stage at which it was transferred.]

20. (1) Save as otherwise provided by any enactment for the time being in
force, an appeal from a decree or order of a District Judge or
District Judge shall lie to the High Court Division.
(2) An appeal shall not lie to the High Court Division from a decree or order
of an District Judge in any case in which, if the decree or order
had been made by the District Judge, an appeal would not lie to that Court.

Section_Title: Reference of Judges in other laws
Content:
25A. Where in any other law for the time being in force, any
reference is made to Additional Judge, or Subordinate Judge, or Senior
Assistant Judge, or Assistant Judge, the reference shall be construed
respectively as to Additional District Judge, or Joint District Judge, or
of Indian Laws) Order, 1937.]

Section_Title: Power to confer powers of Civil Courts
Content:
36. (1) The Government may invest with the powers of any Civil Court under
this Act, by name or in virtue of office,-
(b) after consultation with the High Court Division any officer serving in any
part of the territories to which this Act extends and belonging to a class
defined in this behalf by the Government.
(2) Nothing in sections 4, 5, 6, 8. 10 or 11 applies to any officer so invested,
but all the other provisions of this Act shall, so far as those provisions can be
made applicable, apply to him as if he were a Judge of the Court with the
powers of which he is invested.
XIII of 1962).]
(4) Where the place at which the Court of an officer invested with powers
under sub-section (1) is to be held has not been fixed under section 14, the
Court may be held at any place within the local limits of its jurisdiction.

Section_Title: Certain decisions to be according to local law
Content:
37.(1) Where in any suit or other proceeding it is necessary for a Civil Court to decide any question regarding succession, inheritance, marriage or caste, or any religious usage or institution, the Muslim law in cases where the parties are Muslim, and the Hindu law in cases where the parties are Hindus, shall form the rule of decision except in so far as such law has, by legislative enactment, been altered or abolished.
(2) In cases not provided for by sub-section (1) or by any other law for the time being in force, the Court shall act according to justice, equity and good conscience.

Section_Title: Judges not to try suits in which they are interested
Content:
38. (1) The presiding officer of a Civil Court shall not try any suit or other proceeding to which he is a party or in which he is personally interested.
(2) The presiding officer of an appellate Civil Court under this Act shall not try an appeal against a decree or order passed by himself in another capacity.
(3) When any such suit, proceeding or appeal as is referred to in sub-section (1) or sub-section (2) comes before any such officer, the officer shall forthwith transmit the record of the case to the Court to which he is immediately subordinate, with a report of the circumstances attending the reference.
(4) The superior Court shall thereupon dispose of the case under [section 24 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908].
(5) Nothing in this section shall be deemed to affect the extraordinary original civil jurisdiction of the High Court Division.

Section_Title: Subordination of Courts to District Court
Content:
39. For the purposes of the last foregoing section the presiding officer of a
Court subject to the administrative control of the District Judge shall be
deemed to be immediately subordinate to the Court of the District Judge,
and, for the purposes of the Code of Civil Procedure 1908, the Court of such
an officer shall be deemed to be of a grade inferior to that of the Court of the
District Judge.

Section_Title: Application of Act to Courts of Small Causes
Content:
40.(1) This section and sections 15, 32, 37, 38 and 39 apply to Courts of Small Causes constituted under the  Small Cause Courts Act, 1887.
(2) Save as provided by that Act, the other sections of this Act do not apply to those Courts.
